faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Quick answers to the most common questions.

items.q: How do I invite a tracker?
items.a: Go to Invite tracker, enter the email address, and send the invitation.

items.q: What happens if the link expired?
items.a: Request a new invitation from the organization.

items.q: How do I get access to the app?
items.a: You need an invitation from an Owner/Admin of an organization. If you don't have access, ask them to invite you.

items.q: What is the difference between Owner, Administrator and Tracker?
items.a: Owner manages the organization and administrators. Administrator manages operational data (geofences, personnel, activities, assignments). Tracker is a field user who sends GPS points.

items.q: Can I manage more than one organization?
items.a: Yes. Owners can work with multiple organizations and switch using the organization selector.

items.q: Why do I see "No recent data" in Tracker?
items.a: The tracker may be offline, without location permissions, or without an active assignment. Check permissions and assignments.

items.q: How often is the location sent?
items.a: The frequency is defined in the assignment. The minimum allowed is 5 minutes to preserve battery and data.

items.q: Does it work with a weak signal?
items.a: Yes. The tracker can store points and sync when connectivity returns (depending on your build and permissions).

items.q: How are costs calculated?
items.a: Costs are calculated from assignments (hours) and the hourly rate configured in each activity.

items.q: Can I export to Excel?
items.a: Yes. Reports can be exported as CSV, which you can open in Excel or Google Sheets.

items.q: Can I filter by geofence, person, activity and date range?
items.a: Yes. Use the filters in Reports and the Cost panel to focus by geofence(s), person(s), activity(ies) and dates.

items.q: What location data is stored?
items.a: Only the points needed for tracking and geofence validation. Access is controlled by roles and organization.

items.q: Can a tracker see data from other organizations?
items.a: No. Data is isolated by organization (multi-tenant) and protected by database security rules.

instructions.bestPractice1Body: Always use the same phone to avoid breaks in tracking.
instructions.bestPractice2Body: Keep enough battery or plug in during long shifts.
instructions.bestPractice3Body: Do not sign out or disable location while you are on route.
instructions.bestPractice4Body: If something does not load, re-open your most recent invitation link.
instructions.bestPractice4Title: Re-open the link if it fails
instructions.recommendationBody: Before leaving, make sure you see the "Active tracking" status on your screen.
resultBullets: You granted location permissions on your phone.
You keep internet and location enabled.
If it does not work, you can request a new invitation or contact support.
instructions.resultIntro: When done, you should have everything ready to share your location without issues:

steps.title: Step by step
steps.subtitle: Follow this simple sequence to activate the tracker in a few minutes.

items.title: Receive your invitation
items: You will receive a link by email or message from your organization.

items.title: Open the link on your phone
items: Open it on the same phone you will use for the tracker.

instructions.subtitle: Activate your tracker with simple steps and clear messages, with no technical setup.
instructions.title: Tracker quick guide

toc.title: Table of contents
toc.subtitle: Use these shortcuts to move faster.

support.title: Support
support.subtitle: Contact us for technical support, billing questions, or training.
support.notConfigured: Support contact is not configured yet.

commonProblems.title: Common problems
commonProblems.subtitle: If something is not working, review these quick solutions before contacting support.

items.title: I can’t accept the invitation
items.desc: Open the most recent link you received. If it expired or shows an error, ask for a new invitation.

items.title: The link does not work
items.desc: Copy it completely into your phone browser or open it directly from the original message.

onboarding.title: First steps
onboarding.subtitle: How to start using the tracker for the first time.

items.q: How do I start using the tracker?
items.a: You will receive an invitation link. Just open it from your phone and follow the steps.

items.q: Do I need to install anything?
items.a: No. Just open the link and accept the permissions when prompted.

tracker.title: Location tracking
tracker.subtitle: What it means for tracking to be active.

items.q: What does 'Active tracking' mean?
items.a: It means your location is being shared correctly.

items.q: Do I need to keep the app open?
items.a: Not always. As long as your device has permissions and connection, tracking can continue.

troubleshooting.title: Common problems
troubleshooting.subtitle: Quick solutions for the most common cases.

items.q: I can't accept the invitation
items.a: Make sure you open the most recent link. If it keeps failing, request a new invitation.

items.q: The link says it is inactive
items.a: This can happen if you opened an earlier link. Use the latest link received.

items.q: Tracking is not starting
items.a: Check that you accepted location permissions on your device.

geofences.title: Geofences
geofences.subtitle: What they are and how they work within tracking.

items.q: What is a geofence?
items.a: It is an area defined on the map to record entries, exits or presence.

items.q: Do I have to do anything as a tracker?
items.a: No. The system automatically records your activity when applicable.

account.title: Account and access
account.subtitle: Questions about signing in and accessing the organization.

items.q: How do I sign in?
items.a: Use your email and password from the sign-in screen.

items.q: What do I do if I am in the wrong organization?
items.a: Make sure you use the correct invitation link or sign in again with the indicated account.
